name: Milt Buckner
abstract: Milton Brent Buckner (July 10, 1915 – July 27, 1977) was an American jazz pianist and organist, who in the early 1950s popularized the Hammond organ. He pioneered the parallel chords style that influenced Red Garland, George Shearing, Bill Evans, and Oscar Peterson.

concerts.label: Newport Jazz Festival: Salute to Eddie Condon and Ben Webster

works.t: On a Clear Day You Can See Forever: On a Clear Day (You Can See Forever)

about_quotes.speaker: Tiny Grimes
about_quotes.evidence: In 1968 you took a tour of Europe with Milt Buckner... I had a ball playing with Milt.

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Coleman
about_quotes.evidence: In September, I was engaged for a concert in Antwerp with Ben Webster, Buddy Tate, Jimmy Woody, Wallace Bishop, Milt Buckner and Joe Turner. It was nice to blow with those guys who were all friends.

about_quotes.speaker: Arnett Cobb
about_quotes.evidence: So I got with the guys that do the things I do, like Milton Buckner... And we got to be real buddies... Milton Buckner was one of the main arrangers in the band.

about_quotes.speaker: Jo Jones
about_quotes.evidence: Well, from 1969 I started out with Milt Buckner, an organist, a drifting organist and pianist. I did things with him with piano and things like that.

about_quotes.speaker: Big Nick Nicholas
about_quotes.evidence: I worked there with Milt Buckner one time, on a Monday... It was Panama and Roy Gaines, Milt Buckner and myself.

about_quotes.speaker: Howard McGhee
about_quotes.evidence: He had Milt Buckner and Wardell Gray, myself... Buckner used to write for the band and I'd write for the band.
